definition:
1. to be controller  of receipts and expenditure, to be treasurer, paymaster , (Aristophanes Comicus):—;with genitive, τ. τῆς Παράλου to be paymaster of  the Paralus, (Demosthenes Orator) 
2. at Rome, to be quaestor , (Plutarch) 
3. transitive to deal out, dispense , (Plato Philosophus), etc.:—;Pass., τοὺς νόμους τεταμιεύμεθα we have  the laws dealt out , (Lysias Orator):—;Mid., ταμιεύεσθαι εἰς ὅσον βουλόμεθα ἄρχειν to control  the limits to which we mean to extend our sway, (Thucydides) 
4. of keeping house, to regulate, manage , (Aristophanes Comicus):—; Pass., χώρα ταμιευομένα τινί governed  or possessed  by one, (Pindar) 
5. to store up , (Demosthenes Orator); Ζηνὸς ταμιεύεσκε γονάς she was the depository of  it, (Sophocles Tragicus) (ML)
